content: I've been trying to get back into a fitness routine after moving to Raleigh last month. My border collie Ziggy needs more exercise too.
Welcome to Raleigh! That's a great idea to combine your fitness with Ziggy's exercise needs. Have you thought about what activities you'd like to try?
I'm thinking about mountain biking since I used to do it back in Boston. The trails here look amazing. Also considering joining a gym near MIT's Raleigh campus where I work.
Mountain biking is fantastic here! Umstead State Park has great trails. What's your current fitness level? I can suggest some beginner-friendly routes.
I'd say intermediate. I've been pretty sedentary the last few months with the move and starting my new robotics research position. Need to build back up slowly.
Smart approach. Have you looked into any local mountain biking groups? They often have scheduled rides at different skill levels.
Not yet, but that's a good idea. I'm also trying to eat healthier - maybe cut back on the home brewing experiments. Those beers are delicious but not helping my fitness goals!
Home brewing is such a fun hobby though! Maybe you could try brewing some lighter options or non-alcoholic versions? There are some great recipes out there.
That's true. I could experiment with some low-calorie options. Ziggy would probably appreciate if I spent less time brewing and more time taking him to the dog park.
Absolutely! Raleigh has some great dog parks. Have you been to Millbrook Exchange Park yet? It's one of the largest in the area.
No, I haven't checked that one out yet. I'll add it to our list. I'm also thinking about getting a bike rack for my car so we can explore trails outside the city on weekends.
Great idea! The Mountains-to-Sea Trail has some beautiful sections within driving distance. What kind of car do you have? I can recommend some bike rack options.
I drive a Subaru Outback - perfect for outdoor activities. I'm also curious about trying some of the local Japanese restaurants to balance out the fitness efforts with good food!
The Outback is perfect for this! For Japanese food, you should try Mura in North Hills or Shiki Sushi in Durham. Both are excellent. Have you picked up any photography gear since moving?
Not yet, but I want to start documenting our adventures on the trails. Maybe that'll be my reward after getting back into shape. Ziggy makes a great photography subject too.
He definitely does! Action shots of dogs on trails are so much fun to capture. Let me know if you want any tips on outdoor photography techniques.
Will do! I think I have a solid plan now - mountain biking, dog parks, better eating habits, and maybe some light home brewing experiments. Thanks for the suggestions!
Sounds like a perfect balance! Remember to start slow and build up gradually. I'm excited to hear how your fitness journey goes with Ziggy in beautiful Raleigh!
